questions.question:
Consider a simultaneous game with two players, where Player 1 has the following strategy and payoff matrix: 

|   | A | B 
|----|----|----
| A | (1,1) | (0,5) 
| B | (5,0) | (3,3) 

 Player 1 will play A if the probability of Player 2 playing A is at least 0.3 and will play B otherwise. 

Find the sub-game perfect equilibrium for this game. Explain your reasoning. (20 marks)

A firm produces a product that has two possible features: feature A and feature B. 

|   | Feature A | Feature B 
|----|-----------|-----------
| 0  | (10,0)   | (5,5)    
| 1  | (0,10)   | (8,2)    

Suppose a player (a firm) can choose to produce either feature A or feature B. 

The player's payoffs are such that (assuming a linear utility function) if both players choose feature A, the total payoff is 20. 

If one player chooses A and the other B, then the firm's payoff is 15 if the other firm produces B and 10 if the other firm produces A. 

If one firm chooses A and the other B, then the payoff is 1 for the firm that chose A. 

The firm has a discount factor of 0.9. 

Find the sub-game perfect equilibrium for this game. Explain your reasoning. (20 marks)

In a game of auctions, two bidders (players) are competing for a single item. The bidders have the following payoffs: 

|   | Bid 2 | Bid 3 
|----|-------|-------
| 2  | (10)   | (15)   
| 3  | (20)   | (25)   

The bidders have the following strategies: Bidder 1 will bid 2 if the probability of Bidder 2 bidding 2 is at least 0.5 and will bid 3 otherwise. 

Bidder 2 will bid 2 if the probability of Bidder 1 bidding 2 is at least 0.7 and will bid 3 otherwise. 

Find the sub-game perfect equilibrium for this game. Explain your reasoning. (20 marks)
